description_long: This is a commemorative obelisk featuring a rectangular plaque mounted on a flat, vertical slab. The plaque is metallic and displays a relief portrait of a man's face on the left side, with Cyrillic text and dates inscribed to the right. The slab itself is a solid, pale-colored structure, likely made of concrete or stone, with a smooth, matte finish. The plaque appears weathered, with visible patina and some surface irregularities, suggesting exposure to the elements. The overall design is utilitarian, with the focus on the memorial plaque rather than elaborate ornamentation. The obelisk is likely intended as a public memorial or marker in an urban environment.
description: A pale, rectangular memorial obelisk with a weathered metallic plaque featuring a relief portrait and Cyrillic inscription. The design is simple and functional, emphasizing the commemorative plaque.
description_view_0: Front view showing the metallic plaque with a relief portrait and Cyrillic inscription clearly visible on the pale slab.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the obelisk and the protrusion of the plaque from the slab.
description_view_2: Rear oblique view with the plaque and inscription visible in reverse, showing the slab's flat surface and slight shadowing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the thinness of the structure and the raised plaque.
attribute_inscription: A Cyrillic inscription is present on the metallic plaque, including a name and dates (1930-1945). The text is raised, bold, and legible, located to the right of a relief portrait.
attribute_ornamentation: A relief portrait of a man's face is present on the left side of the plaque, serving as the primary decorative element. No additional ornamentation is visible.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple, flat extension of the vertical slab, with no visible steps, platforms, or decorative features. The material appears to be the same pale concrete or stone as the main structure.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is not visible; the top of the slab is flat and unadorned, with no unique features or decorations.
